subject: First Aid & Safety
title: CPR for Drowning Victims
description: A quick win: understand CPR for Drowning Victims.

hook.text: Drowning CPR is different from cardiac CPR. Start with rescue breaths, not compressions.

buildup.text: In cardiac arrest, the heart fails first. In drowning, breathing fails first—the heart still has oxygen.

discovery.text: Five initial rescue breaths deliver oxygen to a body that's been deprived, not stopped.

twist.text: Standard guidelines say compressions first for all cardiac arrest—but drowning is the exception.

climax.text: After rescue breaths, follow standard CPR cycles until the person breathes or help arrives.

punchline.text: Drowning stops breath first. Give breath first. The logic is simple.

quiz.question: Why should drowning CPR start with rescue breaths instead of compressions?
options: Drowning deprives oxygen first—the heart may still have some
Compressions push water out of the lungs
It's easier to start with breaths
